title: Components and cycle detection

publication.changeSummary: Language-neutral DSA draft with explicit C/C++/Java/Python mapping for Components and cycle detection; independent algorithm, implementation, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: CS2023 Algorithmic Foundations; August 2023; material applicable to Components and cycle detection
sources.claim: Connected components partition undirected reachability, while cycle detection tracks parent or active recursion state according to graph direction.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Connected components partition undirected reachability, while cycle detection tracks parent or active recursion state according to graph direction.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: Directed DFS finds a back edge to an active recursion-stack vertex as cycle evidence. Your goal is to implement and test component counting and one appropriate cycle detector. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Stacks, queues and deques; Hash tables, sets and maps; Breadth-first and depth-first search. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Shared concept and invariant
sections.body: Connected components partition undirected reachability, while cycle detection tracks parent or active recursion state according to graph direction. This claim is assessed independently from syntax so conceptual evidence can transfer.

sections.heading: C, C++, Java, and Python implementation map
sections.body: In C, represent adjacency and disjoint sets with explicit buffers and ownership; C++ uses vector-based lists and standard priority structures; Java uses typed collections and careful object equality; Python uses list/dict/set and heapq, with the same graph or partition invariant and complexity target.

sections.heading: Boundary and common failure
sections.body: Directed and undirected cycle rules differ, and self-loops or parallel edges need declared handling. The draft therefore rejects: Any edge to an already visited vertex proves a cycle in every DFS.

includes: Apply traversal state to connected components and directed or undirected cycle detection.
Implement and test component counting and one appropriate cycle detector.

In C, represent adjacency and disjoint sets with explicit buffers and ownership; C++ uses vector-based lists and standard priority structures; Java uses typed collections and careful object equality; Python uses list/dict/set and heapq, with the same graph or partition invariant and complexity target.
excludes: Directed and undirected cycle rules differ, and self-loops or parallel edges need declared handling.

outcomes: Implement and test component counting and one appropriate cycle detector.

examples.title: Components and cycle detection: language-neutral trace
examples.situation: Directed DFS finds a back edge to an active recursion-stack vertex as cycle evidence.
walkthrough: Step 1 — State the abstract input and invariant: Directed DFS finds a back edge to an active recursion-stack vertex as cycle evidence.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: Cycle detection must distinguish the parent or completed vertices; only the direction-appropriate state proves a cycle.
examples.result: The conceptual result follows because Cycle detection must distinguish the parent or completed vertices; only the direction-appropriate state proves a cycle.

Step 2 — Map storage, library, equality, bounds, ownership, and error rules using: In C, represent adjacency and disjoint sets with explicit buffers and ownership; C++ uses vector-based lists and standard priority structures; Java uses typed collections and careful object equality; Python uses list/dict/set and heapq, with the same graph or partition invariant and complexity target.
